title: T-Operator Coherence PASS: cross-domain evidence (finance + EEG + audio)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates the topographic observer → QA → T-operator pipeline works across three unrelated domains. The observer architecture is domain-general: multi-channel signal → clustering → microstate transitions → QA orbit classification. Each domain validates a different aspect: finance (forward prediction), EEG (contemporaneous classification), audio (structural detection).

qci_spec.definition: rolling T-prediction accuracy (finance) / orbit distribution difference (EEG) / partial autocorrelation (audio)

oos_protocol.split_description: each domain uses appropriate OOS methodology

partial_correlation.interpretation: finance QCI independent of vol persistence

domain_witnesses.mechanism: QCI = rolling T-accuracy; low QCI → high future vol
topographic k-means on 23 EEG channels → orbit distribution differs seizure vs baseline
QA orbit transition rates differ by signal type (sine vs noise)

cross_domain_thesis: The topographic observer → QA state → T-operator coherence pipeline detects genuine structure in multi-channel signals regardless of domain. The T-operator error signal (deviation from Fibonacci-shift predictions) carries information about system stability that domain-specific baselines do not fully capture.
